sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first. excellent oils-and-fats category fit because this is the cleanest flagship example of a strong fat-profile food in the category. contains useful polyphenols because adds value beyond the core fat chemistry alone. very simple ingredient identity because a clear wholefood-adjacent advantage over more industrial fat products.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: on the weaker side. extremely energy dense because still pure fat, so calories accumulate fast. quality varies a lot because source and authenticity matter in practice. easy to overpour without noticing because a healthy image can make portion creep happen fast in practice.

closing.summary: Extra Virgin Olive Oil has a genuinely strong overall case, especially excellent oils-and-fats category fit and contains useful polyphenols. the downsides are there, but extremely energy dense and quality varies a lot do not outweigh the upside. best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.

closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
